rulings.Case Title: NIGEL CHANTER ET AL VS. GREGORY MALIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 10, 2024 line 1. PETITIONER NIGEL CHANTER, LAI CHEUNG, PETER BAUMANNN, ALISON BAUMANN, TON-YUN FANG, GNOMES COVE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, BABAC DOANE NOTICE OF HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is continued to December 26, 2024 for the moving party to provide a courtesy copy of a filed endorsed Memorandum of Points and Authorities and Declaration of Gregory Malin referenced in the November 25, 2024 proof of service. Moving party shall also file a supplemental declaration addressing the differences between the two claims noticed for hearing on December 10, 2024. Moving party may attach a copy of the Claim or lodge a copy of the claim and file a notice of lodgment for both no later than December 13, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DCP SF COLUMBUS AVE OWNER, LLC VS. EXCEL HOTEL SERVICES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 10, 2024 line 2. PLAINTIFF DCP SF COLUMBUS AVE OWNER, LLC MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Plaintiff's motion to strike answer is granted. No opposition filed. Corporate defendant has been unrepresented since May 6, 2024. There is no indication on the Court record (and no opposition filed) suggesting that corporation is obtaining counsel. Plaintiff's motion to enter default against Defendant is denied for Plaintiff to request a default from the clerk of court. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY VAN REES ET AL VS. DEL TORO LOAN SERVICING, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 10, 2024 line 4. PLAINTIFF JEFFREY VAN REES AN INDIVIDUAL, ERICA TORRE / NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is DENIED. Plaintiffs failed to show likely success on the merits for (1) deficiency of notice under the Deed of Trust, and (2) applicability of Civ. Code Section 2923.5 to this mortgage. Plaintiffs also failed to establish irreparable harm. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ESSEX PORTFOLIO LP VS. ALEXEI DIACHOFF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 10, 2024 line 12. DEFENDANT ALEXEI DIACHOFF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / NOTICE OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to provide any evidence that their primary language is not Chinese, Russian and Filipino. Defendant's position that SFRO requires the landlord to serve a copy of the form 1007 in all languages or in English is not supported by the clear language of SFRO or any case authority. "A landlord shall attach a copy of the form that is in the primary language of the tenant to a notice to vacate before serving the notice, except that if the tenant's primary language is not English, Chinese, Spanish, Vietnamese, Tagalog or Russian, the landlord shall attach a copy of the form that is in English to the notice." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
